commentary_text: The model rates Croatia to win at 79% while the sportsbook consensus stands at 54%—a 25-percentage-point gap favoring the model's confidence in a Croatian victory. This substantial divergence suggests the market is pricing in information the model cannot access, most likely recent lineup changes, injury news, or current form that has shifted expectations away from the statistical baseline. The model's Elo-based forecast reflects historical strength but cannot see squad updates or match fitness since its last training cycle. The dashboard surfaces this gap as a signal worth monitoring: either the market has spotted genuine changes in team readiness, or the model's rating advantage will prove durable.
